82af666a7315.title: Explain me about the failure history of the P-101 and about…

messages.content: explain me about the failure history of the P-101 and about its timeline?

**What is happening.** P-101 drive-end vibration is 5.45 mm/s and rising at ~0.83 mm/s/day — inside the alert zone (4.5–7.1 mm/s per SOP-315 [5]). At the current trend it crosses the 7.1 mm/s danger limit in about **2.0 days** (RUL). Bearing temperature is normal (71.4 °C), which points at mechanical wear, not lubrication.

**Why.** Shaft misalignment driving bearing wear (BRG-204): the P&ID shows P-101 coupled to motor M-101, and 3 past work orders on P-101 show the same rising-1x-vibration signature, each resolved by BRG-204 replacement plus laser alignment (vendor manual failure mode 1 [4]).

**Safety — hard gate.** LOTO Permit is REQUIRED before any work: isolate per SAF-12 §3 (breaker MCC-1-04 locked open, valves V-101/V-102 locked closed, zero-energy verified) [8]. No work proceeds without the permit.

**What to do.** Schedule bearing replacement on P-101 within 1 days (predicted danger-limit crossing in ~2.0 days). Isolate per SAF-12 (LOTO), replace 2 x BRG-204 per SOP-207, laser-align, and verify vibration < 2.8 mm/s. Torque housing covers to 45 N·m and verify post-run vibration < 2.8 mm/s per SOP-207 [1]. Spares: 2 × BRG-204 in stock (Store A / Bin 14). A draft work order is prepared (Replace drive-end and non-drive-end bearings (BRG-204) on P-101) — pending your approval.
